progress_summary: Pre-commit gate for the MCP-registry-publish follow-up (new branch claude/mcp-registry-publish off merged main): server.json (official MCP Registry listing, remote streamable-http → opchain.dev/mcp, namespace io.github.asfbay-bit/opchain-skills) + .github/workflows/publish-mcp-registry.yml (publishes on v* tag via GitHub OIDC, no secrets) + CLAUDE.md note. Config/docs only — no app source touched, so tsc/lint/vitest/build are N/A. Meaningful checks ran: server.json valid JSON (node parse OK), workflow YAML parses (python yaml, perms id-token:write), secret scan PASS (OIDC = zero secrets), anti-pattern scan PASS. Verdict PASS.
next_actions: ROOT CAUSE FOUND 2026-06-20: opchain.dev sits behind a Cloudflare MANAGED CHALLENGE ('Just a moment...' bot interstitial) at the edge, BEFORE the worker. Confirmed via deploy-lag canary job logs (run 27837669034): curl to /api/health returned challenge HTML with cType:'managed', cZone:'opchain.dev', /cdn-cgi/challenge-platform on all 3 retries; external fetch got 403. It intercepts ALL non-browser traffic: /api/health (so the deploy-lag canary silently skips every day -> zero drift issues = FALSE comfort) and critically POST /mcp (the entire v1.4.3 Codex endpoint returns a challenge page, not JSON-RPC). Deploys ALWAYS landed (opchain-dev worker modified 2026-06-20 00:45); the front door was bolted, not the code. The prior 'just deploy it / merged-but-undeployed' guidance was WRONG.

FIX (Cloudflare dashboard, user-only — no MCP tool exposes WAF/Bots/zone): Security -> WAF -> Custom rule -> Skip (tick Super Bot Fight Mode + Managed Challenge) for expression (http.host eq "opchain.dev" and (starts_with(http.request.uri.path,"/api/") or http.request.uri.path eq "/mcp")); repeat for staging.opchain.dev. OR Security -> Bots -> disable Bot Fight Mode / Super Bot Fight Mode. Then verify: curl -sS https://opchain.dev/api/health returns JSON with version; curl -sS -X POST https://opchain.dev/mcp -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"tools/list"}' returns JSON-RPC not HTML.

Patch .github/workflows/deploy-lag.yml so the canary detects the 'Just a moment...' challenge body and FAILS LOUDLY (or hits an exempted path) instead of the silent 'no false alarm' skip — it has been blind for days while prod drift went unreported.
After the challenge is lifted, redeploy from latest main (HEAD 892f80e, includes #292 changelog + #294 deploy-decouple) and confirm /api/health version matches git rev-parse --short HEAD.

last_run.note: Merge origin/main into the diagram-refresh branch (no conflicts in the two diagram files; main's non-overlapping changes merged cleanly). Architecture refresh already verified: astro check 0 errors, build 44 pages, vitest 391/391.
